Implement a pure cuBLAS three-SGEMM sequence: SGEMM1 for W@X (opA=N, opB=N), SGEMM2 for X@B (opA=N, opB=T) producing U of shape [d,16] with leading dimension d, and SGEMM3 for A@U with beta=1 accumulating into W@X output.
Ensure SGEMM2 uses m=d, n=16, k=d, and does not materialize B.T explicitly.
Ensure SGEMM3 uses m=d, n=d, k=16, beta=1, and consumes U as stored row-major with ldc=d.
Consider fusing the alpha/beta scaling into the cuBLAS calls to avoid extra scalar launches.

suggested_fixes: Consider using cuBLAS directly to avoid transpose and reduce memory overhead.

The described strategy for the low-rank term (SGEMM2 opA=N/opB=T producing U shape [d,16], then SGEMM3 A@U) is dimensionally inconsistent with the required A@(B.T@X) output.
The small nonzero error at a single d value suggests a subtle dimension or leading-dimension mismatch that only manifests for d=3584.
The qyh-style mapping (SGEMM2: B.T@X with opA=T, opB=N, m=16, n=d, k=d, intermediate Z in [16,d] with ldc=16; SGEMM3: A@Z with beta=1) is the correct pure-cuBLAS approach.

candidate.name: pure_cublas_three_sgemm
candidate.strategy: Replace three separate ATen matmul and addition with three direct cuBLAS SGEMM calls: SGEMM1 W@X (opA=N,opB=N), SGEMM2 X@B (opA=N,opB=T) producing U shape [d,16] with ldc=d, SGEMM3 A@U (opA=N,opB=N) with beta=1 accumulating into Y. Eliminates explicit transpose of B and separate addition kernel.

errors: cuBLAS SGEMM2 and SGEMM3 parameterization for row-major tensors is incorrect: SGEMM2 uses op(B)=T with m=d, n=16, ldc=d, which produces the transpose of the desired intermediate (X @ B.T). This causes SGEMM3 to accumulate the wrong low-rank product, yielding an incorrect Y.
suggested_fixes: Use explicit transpose of B (B.t().contiguous()) and standard op=N, op=N cuBLAS calls with proper dimension ordering, or adopt the common pattern: cublasSgemm(handle, CUBLAS_OP_N, CUBLAS_OP_T, 16, d, d, &alpha, B, 16, X, d, &beta, U, 16) for the intermediate, then cublasSgemm(handle, CUBLAS_OP_N, CUBLAS_OP_N, d, d, 16, &alpha, U, d, A, 16, &beta, Y, d) for accumulation, adjusting leading dimensions accordingly.

name: pure_cublas_three_sgemm
strategy: Replace three separate ATen matmul and addition with three direct cuBLAS SGEMM calls: SGEMM1 W@X (opA=N,opB=N), SGEMM2 X@B (opA=N,opB=T) producing U shape [d,16] with ldc=d, SGEMM3 A@U (opA=N,opB=N) with beta=1 accumulating into Y. Eliminates explicit transpose of B and separate addition kernel.
